q: You had a terrible day. What heals you fastest?
options: A long hug without words
Hearing 'I'm proud of you'
Someone doing the dishes for you
A surprise gift on your pillow
A whole evening together, phones away

q: Your partner forgot your birthday. What stings most?
options: No 'happy birthday' text
No effort to plan anything
No present at all
They didn't take the day off
Not even a hug

q: What makes you feel most loved?
options: When they hold my hand in public
When they write me a real message
When they handle something I was dreading
When they bring me something small and random
When they cancel plans just to be with me
Slow dancing in the kitchen
A handwritten letter
Filling up my gas tank without asking
A playlist made just for me
A whole day with zero distractions

q: You're falling for someone. First sign?
options: I want to be physically close
I start complimenting them constantly
I do things to make their life easier
I notice things they'd like and get them
I lose track of time around them

q: In a fight, what's the fastest way back to good?
options: Just hold me
Say you're sorry and mean it
Show me, don't tell me
A peace offering — flowers, food, something
Sit with me until we're okay

q: What would hurt you most from a partner?
options: Never touching me
Never saying how they feel about me
Letting me handle everything alone
Never thinking of me when they're out
Always being too busy for me

q: How do you show love to your friends?
options: Hugs, arm touches, physical warmth
Hyping them up and saying it out loud
Helping them move, driving them places
Remembering what they mentioned and getting it
Showing up and giving them my time
Waking up tangled together
Sweet morning texts even though we're right here
They made coffee and breakfast already
A little note on the nightstand
Nowhere to be — just us, all morning

q: If love was a language, yours sounds like:
options: Skin to skin
Words that stay with you
Actions louder than words
The thought behind the thing
Undivided presence
